title: Gyeongsang-Style Peppery Korean Dumplings
description: A hearty and invigorating post-workout dish from the Gyeongsang region of Korea, featuring passion fruit pulp and an array of bold spices.
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine the gluten-free flour and water to form a dough, then knead for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 3 inches in diameter.
Place a tablespoon of the passion fruit pulp and a piece of tofu in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a skillet over medium-high heat, and cook the dumplings for 3-4 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, garlic, ginger, cinnamon, salt, and black pepper to the skillet, and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the cooked dumplings to the skillet, and toss to coat with the spicy mixture.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with green onions.
